title: thymus pallidus subsp. cossonianus
intro: Thymus pallidus subsp. cossonianus, also known as the Corsican thyme, is a subspecies of the Thymus pallidus plant, a member of the Lamiaceae family and a type of low-growing, woody shrub native to the Mediterranean region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions. The health benefits of Thymus pallidus subsp. cossonianus include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system, among others. Thymus pallidus subsp. cossonianus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or essential oil, which can be used to relieve coughs, sore throats, and other respiratory issues, as well as to promote relaxation and reduce inflammation.
